extra.reason: Landed on gakatime as a61c7e2. Package-level httpClient{Timeout:60s} replaces http.DefaultClient in getRawJSON — hung wakatime reads can't stall an import job forever. Worker.Cancel now returns (<-chan struct{}, bool); StartJob's defer closes the done channel after the terminal DB write (finishCancelled/finishError), so the /cancel handler waits on it instead of the old time.Sleep(150ms) race — bounded at 5s + request-context so a stuck goroutine can't hang the response. When the job isn't running here, done is pre-closed so callers wait uniformly. Worker.DB/Logger/Hub → db/logger/hub (unexported; NewWorker + StartJob/Cancel are the only public surface). MapState + JobPending/JobFailed/JobFinished + TestMapState deleted (audit-dead). Tests: importer/handler suites green under BOOM_REQUIRE_DB=1; new TestCancelReturnsPreClosedChannelForUnknownJob + TestCancelDoneChannelClosesAfterWorkerExit lock the ack contract.

Landed on gakatime as cacae86. Added EmitAreaLine + EmitDayHeatmap primitives; 4 new twin kinds each ~30 LOC of composition over them: cumulative-area (filled area of accumulating time), deep-work (Sessions summary card w/ Needs{Sessions:true} lazy fetch), heatmap-projects + heatmap-languages (top-6-by-seconds × day intensity grids). FE catalog entries with primitive metadata; EmbedLinkButton wired on Overview 'Cumulative coding time', 'Activity per project', 'Activity per language', 'Deep-work sessions' cards. Drift-guard test now covers 13 kinds. Gates: go build/vet/test ./... green (BOOM_REQUIRE_DB=1); vitest 23 files / 213 tests; typecheck clean. Note: web/node_modules was missing at the target path, ran npm ci; unrelated to the change.

Landed on gakatime. MVP builder — no saved-defs table for v1; the whole composition lives in the URL as ?spec=<base64(json)>. Backend: internal/widget/custom.go with Def{Layout,Panels[]} + PanelKind enum (calendar/area/top-langs/top-projects/grade/punchcard/momentum/metrics) + Layout enum (1/2h/2v/3h); Encode/DecodeDef use base64.RawURLEncoding (std b64 accepted on decode); NeedsForDef aggregates panel data requirements so the handler fetches only what a composition asks for; renderCustom computes panel rects from layout + dispatches each slot to its emit* primitive with missing-data placeholders (no panic). widgets.go handler branches on kind=='custom' to parse the spec + aggregate needs + dispatch to widget.RenderCustom, sharing cache-key + LoadHiddenSets/RenameSets privacy path with fixed kinds. FE: features/widgets/builder.ts mirrors the Go types + url-safe base64 encoder + customWidgetUrl; WidgetBuilder.tsx is a Sheet with layout picker + N panel pickers + range/theme/title + live <object> preview + Markdown/URL copy; Build trigger wired into WidgetsPanel. Tests: internal/widget/custom_test.go round-trips + bad-input matrix + NeedsForDef + three-panel composite fingerprint + missing-data + all 4 layouts; features/widgets/builder.test.ts panelCount + url-safe b64 + URL shape. Gates green: go build/vet/test ./... (BOOM_REQUIRE_DB=1); vitest 24/216; typecheck. Deferred: widget_defs table for naming + revoking a saved composition (URL-inline is fine for one-shot embeds; naming is valuable when iterating on the same widget across README updates).

Landed on gakatime as d3ab6c2. logging.Setup now returns (*slog.Logger, *LogHub); package-global hub + Hub() accessor deleted. server.New + handler.New take *logging.LogHub as an explicit param so wiring reads top-to-bottom in main.go. cmd/boomtime run destructures both; run-migrations discards the hub (no HTTP, no live stream). internal/testutil.NewHarnessWithDB passes nil for logHub — harness router doesn't register the Logs live-stream endpoint and handler/logs.go already guards on nil. Fixes the hidden ordering dependency where handler.New silently reads logging.Hub() and only worked because Setup happened to run first. Full go test ./... green under BOOM_REQUIRE_DB=1; go build/vet clean.

Landed on gakatime as b13708e. Route-lazy'd every authed feature page (Overview, Projects, Leaderboards, Heartbeats, SpaceView, Import, Settings) via React.lazy + <Suspense>; auth pages stay eager (tiny pre-auth surface, first-nav cost). vite.config.ts rolldownOptions.manualChunks partitions node_modules into vendor-d3 / vendor-react / vendor-radix so those long-lived libs cache separately. Result: single 879 kB / 260 kB gzip chunk → index 156 kB / 43 kB gzip + cacheable vendor chunks + per-route 6-80 kB pages. Initial gzipped payload down ~52% (260 → 124 kB). chunkSizeWarningLimit raised to 600 kB and no chunk breaches it. Gates: vitest 24/216 pass, typecheck clean, prod build clean. Playwright's chromium had SEGVs on macOS-arm this run (browser-level, unrelated).

Nothing to do — the doc was already updated in an earlier commit (likely part of the u45 audit landing). Verified every current internal/db/*.go reference in docs/QUERY_ENGINE.md points at the split files: ingest.go (SaveHeartbeats, RefreshRollup), activity.go (GetUserActivity/Rollup), predicates.go (HiddenSets, exclusionPredicate), remap.go (RenameSets, remapExpr), spaces.go (MemberSets, inclusionPredicate), curation.go (rule CRUD), splice.go (applyScopes, injectAfter), axes.go (registry), leaderboards.go, projects.go, bigbets.go, active_files.go, rows.go. No sessions.go references remain (grep confirms). Bead's line numbers (54/137/662/724) were relative to a pre-fix version of the doc — the corresponding sections read correctly today. Closing as already-done, no commit.

Already fixed — landed as part of gaka-e0l (commit 7f5dbea, 'widen hb_rollup_daily to 8 axes'). The Stats handler gate at internal/handler/stats.go:28-30 reads exactly the audit's requested option (a): 'limit == 15 && !HasHiddenOutside(RollupAxes) && (!spaceRequested || !HasMemberOutside(RollupAxes))'. Spaces whose rules only target rollup axes (project/language/editor/platform/machine/category/plugin/branch) now take the fast rollup path; only entity or a future non-rollup axis forces raw. MemberSets.HasMemberOutside is exercised: TestHasMemberOutside (internal/db/spaces_test.go:71) covers both directions (rollup axis → false, entity → true), and the rollup query splices the same inclusion predicate. Closing as already-done, no commit.

manifests + Argo Application + docker-publish + Tiltfile + Image Updater all landed; follow-ups closed except gaka-yjc (waiting on 1P item)
Converged logs WS onto refresh cookie auth; deleted resolveUserFromQueryToken; frontend drops ?token= query param; go build + go test ./internal/handler and vitest logs suite green

Remaining ia3 items shipped: (1) useAuth refresh loop now distinguishes ApiError 401/403 (clear+broadcast) from network/5xx (leave state, retry next tick); (2) api.ts request() now attempts single-flight 401→refresh→retry-once. TS + vitest lib/auth suites green. Item (1) WS token in URL closed by gaka-af5. Item (4) DEPENDENT_KEYS drift already fixed per notes.

Added public /healthz endpoint returning version/branch/commit/buildTime/uptime/dbReachable/schemaVersion. Ldflags stamp main.branch/commit/buildTime via Taskfile+Dockerfile ARG BRANCH/COMMIT/BUILDTIME. DB probe uses 2s timeout; SchemaVersion helper queries goose_db_version. go build + go test ./internal/handler green.

Wired BOOM_GRADE_* env parsing in internal/config/config.go — new getEnvFloat helper + loadGradeConfig() that layers overrides on top of stats.DefaultGradeConfig. Config.Grade holds the resolved calibration; cmd/boomtime applies it to stats.DefaultGradeConfig at boot so downstream stats.Grade() picks it up transparently. All 13 knobs (medians, weights, MinRangeDays) individually tunable. go build + config/stats tests green.

Shipped first-run welcome modal: useFirstRunWelcome hook gates on localStorage flag 'boomtime-welcomed', WelcomeModal is a shadcn Dialog introducing Import/Curation/Spaces/Widgets with two CTAs (Import wakatime data → /app/import, Skip). Mounted at AppShell so it floats over any dashboard route without touching ProtectedRoute. Full vitest suite (216 tests) green.

Shipped named/saved custom widget defs — backend complete. New migration 00018_widget_defs.sql (username, name PK + def_id uuid + spec jsonb + timestamps). DB layer: CreateWidgetDef/GetWidgetDef/GetWidgetDefByName/ListWidgetDefs/UpdateWidgetDef/DeleteWidgetDef. Handlers: auth'd /api/v1/users/current/widget-defs CRUD + PUBLIC /widget/svg/:uuid/named renderer that reuses the RenderCustom path with the persisted spec + owner curation. Extracted widget.ValidateDef so the JSON persistence path reuses the same layout/panel whitelist as URL-inline specs. Cache key includes updated_at.Unix() so edits invalidate immediately. Frontend integration (builder Save/Load UI) is a follow-up. go build + handler/widget tests green.

Revalidated ticket vs current code first (gaka-5mo already banked the rollup-with-space fast path for Stats; ticket option A biggest-win claim was pre-5mo; ticket cited worst-case Teak rules are unanchored so option B alone wouldn't help). Shipped stacked C+B: (C) migration 00019_heartbeats_regex_perf.sql adds pg_trgm extension + GIN(project, branch) so the planner can bitmap-scan for both regex ~ and LIKE, directly addressing the 5.5s Punchcard case with unanchored rules; (B) spaces.rewriteAnchoredRegex rewrites ^literal / ^literal$ patterns to indexable LIKE / = when the body is a safe literal (letters/digits/-_/:), served by the new text_pattern_ops btree on project — falls back to raw ~ for any metachar, so worst case is miss-a-rewrite not mis-scope. Full pattern matrix covered by TestRewriteAnchoredRegex. Full go test ./... green (integration tests exercised the rewrite path in the LIKE branch).

Shipped Entity Explorer + destructive REDACT (not delete). Pivoted on user clarification: rows stay, only entity column is blanked so per-project/language/machine totals are unchanged. Backend: internal/db/entities.go (ListEntitiesByType filters entity <> '', RedactEntities does UPDATE SET entity=''), handler /api/v1/users/current/heartbeats/entities (list) + /entities/redact (POST, ?confirm=redact-entities). active_files.go now also filters entity <> '' so redacted values disappear from top-files. Frontend: new Entity Explorer tab on Heartbeats page, four-way type selector (file/app/domain/url), sortable table with per-row + bulk select, confirm dialog listing the entities to be scrubbed. Cache invalidations touch entitiesByType + hbExplore (grouped-by-entity views) but NOT stats/timeline/derivedStatus (rollup isn't affected). Rare (sender,time_sent) unique-collision on multi-entity batches surfaces as friendly 400. Also caught + fixed a sibling bug flagged mid-flight: the Heartbeats page search box only narrowed leaf-row queries; entity now threads through GroupHeartbeats too. And added the pre-existing gaka-o4m follow-up: DerivedStatusPanel now shows per-index sizes for the heartbeats table with a 'perf' badge on the trgm / text_pattern_ops indexes. go test ./... green, yarn vitest run 216/216 green.

Shipped PluginSetup tab (now the default Settings tab). Shows the exact ~/.wakatime.cfg block with api_url derived from window.location.origin + /api/v1, a 'Generate token' button that mints a per-user API key inline and drops the raw value straight into the snippet (with a shown-once warning + link to the Tokens menu for rename/revoke). Also documents the raw ingest endpoints (POST /heartbeats + /heartbeats.bulk) and Basic-auth convention for custom exporters, plus a link to wakatime.com/plugins. Default tab changed from 'curation' to 'plugin' so first-run users see the ingest URL immediately. Tokens confirmed per-user (auth handler + list/delete/rename are all owner-scoped). tsc + vitest 216/216 green.

Shipped attribution — sidebar footer below version chip + first-run welcome modal footer, both link to github.com/TheBranchDriftCatalyst in a new tab. Low-contrast (muted-foreground/70) so it doesn't compete with nav. tsc + 216/216 vitest green.
